description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body and slightly bulging sides, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by three prominent black metal hoops. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, bordered by the metal bands for added structural integrity. One side of the barrel features a small circular bunghole, typically used for filling or emptying the contents. The metal hoops are evenly spaced and provide both reinforcement and a visual accent to the overall design. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments for storage, decoration, or as a functional receptacle.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a small bunghole on one side, featuring a rustic, reinforced design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bunghole and the three black metal hoops encircling the wooden staves.
description_view_1: Side view with uninterrupted wood grain and metal hoops, no bunghole visible.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to view 1, showing the wood texture and metal bands, no bunghole.
description_view_3: Rear view with continuous wood and metal hoops, no bunghole or other features visible.
